item.title: Doks v0.5
item.description: Please your patience, I’m going on a two weeks vacation. The functionalities are there, this blog post and the documentation will be updated when I return. Thanks!

item.title: Doks v0.4
item.description: Multi level section menu Now you can have a multi level section menu (three levels deep). Available for both the collapsibile section menu and the default one. Works with the breadcrumb option if set. Menus → Auto generated section menu [menu] [menu.section] auto = true collapsibleSidebar = true You now have the option to let Doks auto generate the section menu from the directory folder (tree) structure. No manual configuration needed and respects set weight.

item.title: Doks v0.3
item.description: Better UX navigation No more scrolling to the bottom of a page to get to the next page! The navigation parts (global, section, and page) are now all available at the top of a browser window (all screen sizes). This setup also provides for more room for additional navigation elements (all navigation parts). Menus → Multilingual and i18n support Doks now is fully prepared for multilingual and i18n support. Multilingual Mode →

item.title: Doks v0.2
item.description: Doks child theme Doks is now also released as a npm package, making it available as a dependency in the new Doks child theme. This means it’s super easy to update when a new Doks version is released. Just run npm update — that’s all. Of course, you can still use Doks as a starter, if that fits your use case better. Create a new site → Syntax highlighting // Default Doks style [ { "title": "apples", "count": [12000, 20000], "description": {"text": ".

item.title: Say hello to Doks
item.description: Use the standard setup or easily make it your own. Demo doks.netlify.app Build on Hyas Get all the Hyas goodness: Security aware Fast by default ⚡ SEO-ready Development tools (linting, cleaning) Bootstrap framework Netlify-ready (functions, redirects, headers) Plus Full text search — using FlexSearch 💚 Page layouts (landing page, blog, documentation layout) Dark mode — switch to a low-light UI with the click of a button And 100 scores on Google Lighthouse A+ scores on Mozilla Observatory Get started There are two main ways to get started with Doks:
